he.text: Sañjaya said The Supreme Lord Madhusūdan then spoke to the saddened Arjuna whose heart was filled with pity his eyes brimming with tears

ar.text: Sañjaya said The Supreme Lord Madhusūdan then spoke to the saddened Arjuna whose heart was filled with pity his eyes brimming with tears

tr.text: Sañjaya said The Supreme Lord Madhusūdan then spoke to the saddened Arjuna whose heart was filled with pity his eyes brimming with tears

sw.text: Sañjaya said The Supreme Lord Madhusūdan then spoke to the saddened Arjuna whose heart was filled with pity his eyes brimming with tears

he.text: The Supreme Lord said O Arjuna how has such illusion overcome you at this crucial moment This does not befit an Āryan and it leads not to heaven but to infamy

ar.text: The Supreme Lord said O Arjuna how has such illusion overcome you at this crucial moment This does not befit an Āryan and it leads not to heaven but to infamy

tr.text: The Supreme Lord said O Arjuna how has such illusion overcome you at this crucial moment This does not befit an Āryan and it leads not to heaven but to infamy

sw.text: The Supreme Lord said O Arjuna how has such illusion overcome you at this crucial moment This does not befit an Āryan and it leads not to heaven but to infamy

he.text: O son of Kuntī give up this cowardice for it does not befit you O great hero cast off this petty weakness of heart and arise for battle

ar.text: O son of Kuntī give up this cowardice for it does not befit you O great hero cast off this petty weakness of heart and arise for battle

tr.text: O son of Kuntī give up this cowardice for it does not befit you O great hero cast off this petty weakness of heart and arise for battle

sw.text: O son of Kuntī give up this cowardice for it does not befit you O great hero cast off this petty weakness of heart and arise for battle

he.text: Arjuna said O Madhusūdan slayer of the enemy how can I counterattack shooting arrows at venerable Bhīṣma and my teacher Droṇa who are worthy of my worship

ar.text: Arjuna said O Madhusūdan slayer of the enemy how can I counterattack shooting arrows at venerable Bhīṣma and my teacher Droṇa who are worthy of my worship

tr.text: Arjuna said O Madhusūdan slayer of the enemy how can I counterattack shooting arrows at venerable Bhīṣma and my teacher Droṇa who are worthy of my worship

sw.text: Arjuna said O Madhusūdan slayer of the enemy how can I counterattack shooting arrows at venerable Bhīṣma and my teacher Droṇa who are worthy of my worship

he.text: It is better to live in this world and beg without taking the lives of our noble elders and teachers Otherwise by killing them we shall remain in this world only to enjoy their wealth and possessions tainted with their blood

ar.text: It is better to live in this world and beg without taking the lives of our noble elders and teachers Otherwise by killing them we shall remain in this world only to enjoy their wealth and possessions tainted with their blood

tr.text: It is better to live in this world and beg without taking the lives of our noble elders and teachers Otherwise by killing them we shall remain in this world only to enjoy their wealth and possessions tainted with their blood

sw.text: It is better to live in this world and beg without taking the lives of our noble elders and teachers Otherwise by killing them we shall remain in this world only to enjoy their wealth and possessions tainted with their blood

he.text: I cannot understand which will be better—victory or defeat for if we kill the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra who now stand before us on the battlefront we shall not wish to live

ar.text: I cannot understand which will be better—victory or defeat for if we kill the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra who now stand before us on the battlefront we shall not wish to live

tr.text: I cannot understand which will be better—victory or defeat for if we kill the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra who now stand before us on the battlefront we shall not wish to live

sw.text: I cannot understand which will be better—victory or defeat for if we kill the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra who now stand before us on the battlefront we shall not wish to live

he.text: Now I am bewildered What is my real duty I am losing heart and distraught So please tell me clearly what is the best course of action to take I am Your surrendered disciple please instruct me

ar.text: Now I am bewildered What is my real duty I am losing heart and distraught So please tell me clearly what is the best course of action to take I am Your surrendered disciple please instruct me

tr.text: Now I am bewildered What is my real duty I am losing heart and distraught So please tell me clearly what is the best course of action to take I am Your surrendered disciple please instruct me

sw.text: Now I am bewildered What is my real duty I am losing heart and distraught So please tell me clearly what is the best course of action to take I am Your surrendered disciple please instruct me

he.text: Even if we gain an unrivalled flourishing kingdom on earth and sovereignty over heaven I see nothing to dispel my debilitating grief

ar.text: Even if we gain an unrivalled flourishing kingdom on earth and sovereignty over heaven I see nothing to dispel my debilitating grief

tr.text: Even if we gain an unrivalled flourishing kingdom on earth and sovereignty over heaven I see nothing to dispel my debilitating grief

sw.text: Even if we gain an unrivalled flourishing kingdom on earth and sovereignty over heaven I see nothing to dispel my debilitating grief

he.text: Sañjaya said Thus the valiant vigilant Arjuna spoke to Kṛṣṇa who is Hṛṣīkeś the Lord of the senses of all beings Then he declared “Govinda I will not fight” and fell silent

ar.text: Sañjaya said Thus the valiant vigilant Arjuna spoke to Kṛṣṇa who is Hṛṣīkeś the Lord of the senses of all beings Then he declared “Govinda I will not fight” and fell silent

tr.text: Sañjaya said Thus the valiant vigilant Arjuna spoke to Kṛṣṇa who is Hṛṣīkeś the Lord of the senses of all beings Then he declared “Govinda I will not fight” and fell silent

sw.text: Sañjaya said Thus the valiant vigilant Arjuna spoke to Kṛṣṇa who is Hṛṣīkeś the Lord of the senses of all beings Then he declared “Govinda I will not fight” and fell silent

he.text: O Bhārat there amidst the armies Śrī Hṛṣīkeś smiling spoke thus to the grief-stricken Arjuna

ar.text: O Bhārat there amidst the armies Śrī Hṛṣīkeś smiling spoke thus to the grief-stricken Arjuna

tr.text: O Bhārat there amidst the armies Śrī Hṛṣīkeś smiling spoke thus to the grief-stricken Arjuna

sw.text: O Bhārat there amidst the armies Śrī Hṛṣīkeś smiling spoke thus to the grief-stricken Arjuna

he.text: The Lord said O Arjuna you are lamenting for that which is unworthy of grief yet speaking words of חוכמה The wise lament neither for the living nor the dead

ar.text: The Lord said O Arjuna you are lamenting for that which is unworthy of grief yet speaking words of الحكمة The wise lament neither for the living nor the dead

tr.text: The Lord said O Arjuna you are lamenting for that which is unworthy of grief yet speaking words of bilgelik The wise lament neither for the living nor the dead

sw.text: The Lord said O Arjuna you are lamenting for that which is unworthy of grief yet speaking words of busara The wise lament neither for the living nor the dead

he.text: Never was there a time when you I or all these kings did not exist Just as we exist in the present so have we existed in the past and shall continue to exist in the future

ar.text: Never was there a time when you I or all these kings did not exist Just as we exist in the present so have we existed in the past and shall continue to exist in the future

tr.text: Never was there a time when you I or all these kings did not exist Just as we exist in the present so have we existed in the past and shall continue to exist in the future

sw.text: Never was there a time when you I or all these kings did not exist Just as we exist in the present so have we existed in the past and shall continue to exist in the future

he.text: As the living being passes through the bodily changes of childhood youth and old age it similarly attains another body at death The wise are not deluded by this

ar.text: As the living being passes through the bodily changes of childhood youth and old age it similarly attains another body at death The wise are not deluded by this

tr.text: As the living being passes through the bodily changes of childhood youth and old age it similarly attains another body at death The wise are not deluded by this

sw.text: As the living being passes through the bodily changes of childhood youth and old age it similarly attains another body at death The wise are not deluded by this

he.text: O son of Kuntī the engagement of the senses with their objects produces the sensations of cold heat pleasure and pain But these effects are temporary—they come and go Therefore O Bhārat you must endure them

ar.text: O son of Kuntī the engagement of the senses with their objects produces the sensations of cold heat pleasure and pain But these effects are temporary—they come and go Therefore O Bhārat you must endure them

tr.text: O son of Kuntī the engagement of the senses with their objects produces the sensations of cold heat pleasure and pain But these effects are temporary—they come and go Therefore O Bhārat you must endure them

sw.text: O son of Kuntī the engagement of the senses with their objects produces the sensations of cold heat pleasure and pain But these effects are temporary—they come and go Therefore O Bhārat you must endure them

he.text: O noblest of men the wise man who is equipoised in pleasure and pain and undisturbed by sense-experiences is alone qualified for immortality

ar.text: O noblest of men the wise man who is equipoised in pleasure and pain and undisturbed by sense-experiences is alone qualified for immortality

tr.text: O noblest of men the wise man who is equipoised in pleasure and pain and undisturbed by sense-experiences is alone qualified for immortality

sw.text: O noblest of men the wise man who is equipoised in pleasure and pain and undisturbed by sense-experiences is alone qualified for immortality

he.text: Of the asat destructible object such as the mutable body and associated phenomena there is no eternal existence of the sat eternal object the soul there is no destruction or change The nature of each has been realised by the seers of the truth

ar.text: Of the asat destructible object such as the mutable body and associated phenomena there is no eternal existence of the sat eternal object the soul there is no destruction or change The nature of each has been realised by the seers of the truth

tr.text: Of the asat destructible object such as the mutable body and associated phenomena there is no eternal existence of the sat eternal object the soul there is no destruction or change The nature of each has been realised by the seers of the truth

sw.text: Of the asat destructible object such as the mutable body and associated phenomena there is no eternal existence of the sat eternal object the soul there is no destruction or change The nature of each has been realised by the seers of the truth

he.text: Know that the soul by which the entire body is pervaded is indestructible No one can destroy the imperishable soul

ar.text: Know that the soul by which the entire body is pervaded is indestructible No one can destroy the imperishable soul

tr.text: Know that the soul by which the entire body is pervaded is indestructible No one can destroy the imperishable soul

sw.text: Know that the soul by which the entire body is pervaded is indestructible No one can destroy the imperishable soul

he.text: The soul is said to be eternal indestructible and immeasurable Only these physical bodies are subject to destruction Therefore fight O Bhārat

ar.text: The soul is said to be eternal indestructible and immeasurable Only these physical bodies are subject to destruction Therefore fight O Bhārat

tr.text: The soul is said to be eternal indestructible and immeasurable Only these physical bodies are subject to destruction Therefore fight O Bhārat

sw.text: The soul is said to be eternal indestructible and immeasurable Only these physical bodies are subject to destruction Therefore fight O Bhārat

he.text: Those who think the living being slays and those who think it is slain are ignorant of the true nature of the soul for the soul neither slays nor is slain

ar.text: Those who think the living being slays and those who think it is slain are ignorant of the true nature of the soul for the soul neither slays nor is slain

tr.text: Those who think the living being slays and those who think it is slain are ignorant of the true nature of the soul for the soul neither slays nor is slain

sw.text: Those who think the living being slays and those who think it is slain are ignorant of the true nature of the soul for the soul neither slays nor is slain

he.text: The soul is neither born nor dies it has neither been nor will it be created because it is unborn and eternal It is ever-youthful yet ancient It is not destroyed when the body is destroyed

ar.text: The soul is neither born nor dies it has neither been nor will it be created because it is unborn and eternal It is ever-youthful yet ancient It is not destroyed when the body is destroyed

tr.text: The soul is neither born nor dies it has neither been nor will it be created because it is unborn and eternal It is ever-youthful yet ancient It is not destroyed when the body is destroyed

sw.text: The soul is neither born nor dies it has neither been nor will it be created because it is unborn and eternal It is ever-youthful yet ancient It is not destroyed when the body is destroyed

he.text: O Pārtha knowing the soul to be indestructible eternal birthless and immutable how can a person kill or cause anyone to be killed

ar.text: O Pārtha knowing the soul to be indestructible eternal birthless and immutable how can a person kill or cause anyone to be killed

tr.text: O Pārtha knowing the soul to be indestructible eternal birthless and immutable how can a person kill or cause anyone to be killed

sw.text: O Pārtha knowing the soul to be indestructible eternal birthless and immutable how can a person kill or cause anyone to be killed

he.text: As a person adopts new garments discarding those that are old and worn similarly the soul continues to adopt new bodies leaving those that are old and useless

ar.text: As a person adopts new garments discarding those that are old and worn similarly the soul continues to adopt new bodies leaving those that are old and useless

tr.text: As a person adopts new garments discarding those that are old and worn similarly the soul continues to adopt new bodies leaving those that are old and useless

sw.text: As a person adopts new garments discarding those that are old and worn similarly the soul continues to adopt new bodies leaving those that are old and useless

he.text: Weapons cannot pierce the soul and fire cannot burn it Water cannot wet it and air cannot dry it

ar.text: Weapons cannot pierce the soul and fire cannot burn it Water cannot wet it and air cannot dry it

tr.text: Weapons cannot pierce the soul and fire cannot burn it Water cannot wet it and air cannot dry it

sw.text: Weapons cannot pierce the soul and fire cannot burn it Water cannot wet it and air cannot dry it

he.text: It is said that the soul is indivisible and cannot be burned moistened or dried It is eternal it reaches all planes yet remains still immovable and ever-present

ar.text: It is said that the soul is indivisible and cannot be burned moistened or dried It is eternal it reaches all planes yet remains still immovable and ever-present

tr.text: It is said that the soul is indivisible and cannot be burned moistened or dried It is eternal it reaches all planes yet remains still immovable and ever-present

sw.text: It is said that the soul is indivisible and cannot be burned moistened or dried It is eternal it reaches all planes yet remains still immovable and ever-present

he.text: It is imperceptible inconceivable and untransformable by the sixfold transformations* Therefore knowing the nature of the soul as such there is no reason to lament for it

ar.text: It is imperceptible inconceivable and untransformable by the sixfold transformations* Therefore knowing the nature of the soul as such there is no reason to lament for it

tr.text: It is imperceptible inconceivable and untransformable by the sixfold transformations* Therefore knowing the nature of the soul as such there is no reason to lament for it

sw.text: It is imperceptible inconceivable and untransformable by the sixfold transformations* Therefore knowing the nature of the soul as such there is no reason to lament for it

he.text: And O best of warriors even if you think that the soul is perpetually subject to birth and death you still have no reason to lament

ar.text: And O best of warriors even if you think that the soul is perpetually subject to birth and death you still have no reason to lament

tr.text: And O best of warriors even if you think that the soul is perpetually subject to birth and death you still have no reason to lament

sw.text: And O best of warriors even if you think that the soul is perpetually subject to birth and death you still have no reason to lament

he.text: For one who is born death is certain and for one who dies he must be reborn to undergo the reactions of his past actions Therefore you should not lament over the inevitable

ar.text: For one who is born death is certain and for one who dies he must be reborn to undergo the reactions of his past actions Therefore you should not lament over the inevitable

tr.text: For one who is born death is certain and for one who dies he must be reborn to undergo the reactions of his past actions Therefore you should not lament over the inevitable

sw.text: For one who is born death is certain and for one who dies he must be reborn to undergo the reactions of his past actions Therefore you should not lament over the inevitable

he.text: O Bhārat when all living beings are unmanifest before birth manifest between birth and death and again unmanifest at death why lament for them

ar.text: O Bhārat when all living beings are unmanifest before birth manifest between birth and death and again unmanifest at death why lament for them

tr.text: O Bhārat when all living beings are unmanifest before birth manifest between birth and death and again unmanifest at death why lament for them

sw.text: O Bhārat when all living beings are unmanifest before birth manifest between birth and death and again unmanifest at death why lament for them

he.text: Some see the soul as astonishing some describe it as astonishing some hear of it as astonishing while others though hearing about it know nothing of it

ar.text: Some see the soul as astonishing some describe it as astonishing some hear of it as astonishing while others though hearing about it know nothing of it

tr.text: Some see the soul as astonishing some describe it as astonishing some hear of it as astonishing while others though hearing about it know nothing of it

sw.text: Some see the soul as astonishing some describe it as astonishing some hear of it as astonishing while others though hearing about it know nothing of it

he.text: O Bhārat the soul dwelling within the bodies of all living beings is eternal and cannot be slain Therefore you need lament for no one

ar.text: O Bhārat the soul dwelling within the bodies of all living beings is eternal and cannot be slain Therefore you need lament for no one

tr.text: O Bhārat the soul dwelling within the bodies of all living beings is eternal and cannot be slain Therefore you need lament for no one

sw.text: O Bhārat the soul dwelling within the bodies of all living beings is eternal and cannot be slain Therefore you need lament for no one

he.text: Moreover considering your natural duty you have no reason to waver since there is no action more auspicious for a warrior than a battle for justice

ar.text: Moreover considering your natural duty you have no reason to waver since there is no action more auspicious for a warrior than a battle for justice

tr.text: Moreover considering your natural duty you have no reason to waver since there is no action more auspicious for a warrior than a battle for justice

sw.text: Moreover considering your natural duty you have no reason to waver since there is no action more auspicious for a warrior than a battle for justice

he.text: O Pārtha only the most fortunate warriors are blessed with the opportunity of such a battle which has come to you like an open door to heaven

ar.text: O Pārtha only the most fortunate warriors are blessed with the opportunity of such a battle which has come to you like an open door to heaven

tr.text: O Pārtha only the most fortunate warriors are blessed with the opportunity of such a battle which has come to you like an open door to heaven

sw.text: O Pārtha only the most fortunate warriors are blessed with the opportunity of such a battle which has come to you like an open door to heaven

he.text: But if you choose not to fight this battle for justice your religious principles will be lost fame will desert you and sin will overcome you

ar.text: But if you choose not to fight this battle for justice your religious principles will be lost fame will desert you and sin will overcome you

tr.text: But if you choose not to fight this battle for justice your religious principles will be lost fame will desert you and sin will overcome you

sw.text: But if you choose not to fight this battle for justice your religious principles will be lost fame will desert you and sin will overcome you

he.text: People will speak of your infamy for all time to come and for the renowned infamy is worse than death

ar.text: People will speak of your infamy for all time to come and for the renowned infamy is worse than death

tr.text: People will speak of your infamy for all time to come and for the renowned infamy is worse than death

sw.text: People will speak of your infamy for all time to come and for the renowned infamy is worse than death

he.text: The great warriors who highly honour you will ridicule you considering that you were afraid to fight

ar.text: The great warriors who highly honour you will ridicule you considering that you were afraid to fight

tr.text: The great warriors who highly honour you will ridicule you considering that you were afraid to fight

sw.text: The great warriors who highly honour you will ridicule you considering that you were afraid to fight

he.text: Your enemies will mock you What could be more painful

ar.text: Your enemies will mock you What could be more painful

tr.text: Your enemies will mock you What could be more painful

sw.text: Your enemies will mock you What could be more painful

he.text: O son of Kuntī if you are killed you will attain heaven and if you are victorious you will enjoy your life on earth Therefore being confident of your success arise for battle

ar.text: O son of Kuntī if you are killed you will attain heaven and if you are victorious you will enjoy your life on earth Therefore being confident of your success arise for battle

tr.text: O son of Kuntī if you are killed you will attain heaven and if you are victorious you will enjoy your life on earth Therefore being confident of your success arise for battle

sw.text: O son of Kuntī if you are killed you will attain heaven and if you are victorious you will enjoy your life on earth Therefore being confident of your success arise for battle

he.text: Considering pleasure and pain gain and loss and victory and defeat to be one and the same—fight No sin will be incurred by you

ar.text: Considering pleasure and pain gain and loss and victory and defeat to be one and the same—fight No sin will be incurred by you

tr.text: Considering pleasure and pain gain and loss and victory and defeat to be one and the same—fight No sin will be incurred by you

sw.text: Considering pleasure and pain gain and loss and victory and defeat to be one and the same—fight No sin will be incurred by you

he.text: I have revealed to you this ידע buddhi of the soul Now hear from Me how to act in this ידע bhakti-yoga through which you will be able to free yourself from the bondage of action

ar.text: I have revealed to you this المعرفة buddhi of the soul Now hear from Me how to act in this المعرفة bhakti-yoga through which you will be able to free yourself from the bondage of action

tr.text: I have revealed to you this bilgi buddhi of the soul Now hear from Me how to act in this bilgi bhakti-yoga through which you will be able to free yourself from the bondage of action

sw.text: I have revealed to you this ujuzi buddhi of the soul Now hear from Me how to act in this ujuzi bhakti-yoga through which you will be able to free yourself from the bondage of action

he.text: On this path there can be no loss and no harm The slightest practice of such a spiritual ideal saves one from the greatest fear

ar.text: On this path there can be no loss and no harm The slightest practice of such a spiritual ideal saves one from the greatest fear

tr.text: On this path there can be no loss and no harm The slightest practice of such a spiritual ideal saves one from the greatest fear

sw.text: On this path there can be no loss and no harm The slightest practice of such a spiritual ideal saves one from the greatest fear

he.text: O descendant of the Kuru dynasty resolute intelligence dedicated to Me is one-pointed—I am its only objective But irresolute intelligence is splayed by endless desires for mundane enjoyment

ar.text: O descendant of the Kuru dynasty resolute intelligence dedicated to Me is one-pointed—I am its only objective But irresolute intelligence is splayed by endless desires for mundane enjoyment

tr.text: O descendant of the Kuru dynasty resolute intelligence dedicated to Me is one-pointed—I am its only objective But irresolute intelligence is splayed by endless desires for mundane enjoyment

sw.text: O descendant of the Kuru dynasty resolute intelligence dedicated to Me is one-pointed—I am its only objective But irresolute intelligence is splayed by endless desires for mundane enjoyment

he.text: O Pārtha unwise persons not knowing that the chief directive of the Vedas is the attainment of the Supreme Truth are fond of interpreting the fleetingly enchanting words of the karma-kāṇḍa section of the Vedas as ultimate

ar.text: O Pārtha unwise persons not knowing that the chief directive of the Vedas is the attainment of the Supreme Truth are fond of interpreting the fleetingly enchanting words of the karma-kāṇḍa section of the Vedas as ultimate

tr.text: O Pārtha unwise persons not knowing that the chief directive of the Vedas is the attainment of the Supreme Truth are fond of interpreting the fleetingly enchanting words of the karma-kāṇḍa section of the Vedas as ultimate

sw.text: O Pārtha unwise persons not knowing that the chief directive of the Vedas is the attainment of the Supreme Truth are fond of interpreting the fleetingly enchanting words of the karma-kāṇḍa section of the Vedas as ultimate

he.text: Their hearts are filled with desires and their goal is heaven They advocate the many sacrifices and rituals that yield wealth worldly pleasures and high birth

ar.text: Their hearts are filled with desires and their goal is heaven They advocate the many sacrifices and rituals that yield wealth worldly pleasures and high birth

tr.text: Their hearts are filled with desires and their goal is heaven They advocate the many sacrifices and rituals that yield wealth worldly pleasures and high birth

sw.text: Their hearts are filled with desires and their goal is heaven They advocate the many sacrifices and rituals that yield wealth worldly pleasures and high birth

he.text: Thus attached to enjoyment and opulence deluded by these flowery words of the Vedas they do not attain the resolve to dedicate themselves to the Supreme Lord

ar.text: Thus attached to enjoyment and opulence deluded by these flowery words of the Vedas they do not attain the resolve to dedicate themselves to the Supreme Lord

tr.text: Thus attached to enjoyment and opulence deluded by these flowery words of the Vedas they do not attain the resolve to dedicate themselves to the Supreme Lord

sw.text: Thus attached to enjoyment and opulence deluded by these flowery words of the Vedas they do not attain the resolve to dedicate themselves to the Supreme Lord

he.text: O Arjuna the Vedas deal with the three modes of material nature by accommodating fruitive action and liberation as an allowance and incentive for persons who are ignorant of the true objective of the Vedas But you Arjuna give up duality and all pursuits for gain and preservation stand ever in the plane of truth and by the yoga of חוכמה transcend the modes of nature

ar.text: O Arjuna the Vedas deal with the three modes of material nature by accommodating fruitive action and liberation as an allowance and incentive for persons who are ignorant of the true objective of the Vedas But you Arjuna give up duality and all pursuits for gain and preservation stand ever in the plane of truth and by the yoga of الحكمة transcend the modes of nature

tr.text: O Arjuna the Vedas deal with the three modes of material nature by accommodating fruitive action and liberation as an allowance and incentive for persons who are ignorant of the true objective of the Vedas But you Arjuna give up duality and all pursuits for gain and preservation stand ever in the plane of truth and by the yoga of bilgelik transcend the modes of nature

sw.text: O Arjuna the Vedas deal with the three modes of material nature by accommodating fruitive action and liberation as an allowance and incentive for persons who are ignorant of the true objective of the Vedas But you Arjuna give up duality and all pursuits for gain and preservation stand ever in the plane of truth and by the yoga of busara transcend the modes of nature

he.text: As all the purposes served by several ponds can be served better by a large lake similarly the fruits of worshipping the various gods by their respective Vedic prayers are surpassed by serving Me The knower of Brahma who thus knows the purport of the Vedas fulfils all life’s objectives

ar.text: As all the purposes served by several ponds can be served better by a large lake similarly the fruits of worshipping the various gods by their respective Vedic prayers are surpassed by serving Me The knower of Brahma who thus knows the purport of the Vedas fulfils all life’s objectives

tr.text: As all the purposes served by several ponds can be served better by a large lake similarly the fruits of worshipping the various gods by their respective Vedic prayers are surpassed by serving Me The knower of Brahma who thus knows the purport of the Vedas fulfils all life’s objectives

sw.text: As all the purposes served by several ponds can be served better by a large lake similarly the fruits of worshipping the various gods by their respective Vedic prayers are surpassed by serving Me The knower of Brahma who thus knows the purport of the Vedas fulfils all life’s objectives

he.text: Your right is to your work never to the fruits Be neither motivated by the fruit of action nor inclined to give up action

ar.text: Your right is to your work never to the fruits Be neither motivated by the fruit of action nor inclined to give up action

tr.text: Your right is to your work never to the fruits Be neither motivated by the fruit of action nor inclined to give up action

sw.text: Your right is to your work never to the fruits Be neither motivated by the fruit of action nor inclined to give up action

he.text: O Dhanañjaya give up the ego that you are the doer and be equipoised in success and failure Thus stand firm in the plane of yoga and do your prescribed duties Such a state of balance is indeed known as yoga

ar.text: O Dhanañjaya give up the ego that you are the doer and be equipoised in success and failure Thus stand firm in the plane of yoga and do your prescribed duties Such a state of balance is indeed known as yoga

tr.text: O Dhanañjaya give up the ego that you are the doer and be equipoised in success and failure Thus stand firm in the plane of yoga and do your prescribed duties Such a state of balance is indeed known as yoga

sw.text: O Dhanañjaya give up the ego that you are the doer and be equipoised in success and failure Thus stand firm in the plane of yoga and do your prescribed duties Such a state of balance is indeed known as yoga

he.text: O Dhanañjaya fruitive action is far inferior to this yoga of חוכמה those motivated by the fruits of their actions are misers Therefore seek refuge in the selfless חוכמה of equanimity

ar.text: O Dhanañjaya fruitive action is far inferior to this yoga of الحكمة those motivated by the fruits of their actions are misers Therefore seek refuge in the selfless الحكمة of equanimity

tr.text: O Dhanañjaya fruitive action is far inferior to this yoga of bilgelik those motivated by the fruits of their actions are misers Therefore seek refuge in the selfless bilgelik of equanimity

sw.text: O Dhanañjaya fruitive action is far inferior to this yoga of busara those motivated by the fruits of their actions are misers Therefore seek refuge in the selfless busara of equanimity

he.text: The wise person abstains from both good and bad actions in this world Engage thus in yoga as yoga is the art of all works

ar.text: The wise person abstains from both good and bad actions in this world Engage thus in yoga as yoga is the art of all works

tr.text: The wise person abstains from both good and bad actions in this world Engage thus in yoga as yoga is the art of all works

sw.text: The wise person abstains from both good and bad actions in this world Engage thus in yoga as yoga is the art of all works

he.text: The wise are enlightened by giving up the fruits of their actions and thus attaining liberation from the bondage of birth they reach the plane beyond all suffering

ar.text: The wise are enlightened by giving up the fruits of their actions and thus attaining liberation from the bondage of birth they reach the plane beyond all suffering

tr.text: The wise are enlightened by giving up the fruits of their actions and thus attaining liberation from the bondage of birth they reach the plane beyond all suffering

sw.text: The wise are enlightened by giving up the fruits of their actions and thus attaining liberation from the bondage of birth they reach the plane beyond all suffering

he.text: When your intelligence fully emerges from the dense forest of illusion you will be indifferent to all that has been heard or is yet to be heard

ar.text: When your intelligence fully emerges from the dense forest of illusion you will be indifferent to all that has been heard or is yet to be heard

tr.text: When your intelligence fully emerges from the dense forest of illusion you will be indifferent to all that has been heard or is yet to be heard

sw.text: When your intelligence fully emerges from the dense forest of illusion you will be indifferent to all that has been heard or is yet to be heard

he.text: You shall attain perfect yoga or pure מסירות when your intelligence undisturbed by the apparent contradictions of the Vedas abides in the Lord

ar.text: You shall attain perfect yoga or pure التفاني when your intelligence undisturbed by the apparent contradictions of the Vedas abides in the Lord

tr.text: You shall attain perfect yoga or pure bağlılık when your intelligence undisturbed by the apparent contradictions of the Vedas abides in the Lord

sw.text: You shall attain perfect yoga or pure ujitoaji when your intelligence undisturbed by the apparent contradictions of the Vedas abides in the Lord

he.text: Arjuna said O Keśava what are the characteristics of the person of firmly established חוכמה who is fully absorbed in consciousness of the Supreme samādhi How does such a man of steady mind speak be and act in this world

ar.text: Arjuna said O Keśava what are the characteristics of the person of firmly established الحكمة who is fully absorbed in consciousness of the Supreme samādhi How does such a man of steady mind speak be and act in this world

tr.text: Arjuna said O Keśava what are the characteristics of the person of firmly established bilgelik who is fully absorbed in consciousness of the Supreme samādhi How does such a man of steady mind speak be and act in this world

sw.text: Arjuna said O Keśava what are the characteristics of the person of firmly established busara who is fully absorbed in consciousness of the Supreme samādhi How does such a man of steady mind speak be and act in this world

he.text: The Supreme Lord said O Pārtha one who rejects all desires that enter the mind and remains self-satisfied within is known as a person whose חוכמה is firmly established

ar.text: The Supreme Lord said O Pārtha one who rejects all desires that enter the mind and remains self-satisfied within is known as a person whose الحكمة is firmly established

tr.text: The Supreme Lord said O Pārtha one who rejects all desires that enter the mind and remains self-satisfied within is known as a person whose bilgelik is firmly established

sw.text: The Supreme Lord said O Pārtha one who rejects all desires that enter the mind and remains self-satisfied within is known as a person whose busara is firmly established

he.text: One who is undisturbed by sorrow whose hankering for pleasure has gone and who is free from attachment fear and anger is known as a silent sage of steady mind

ar.text: One who is undisturbed by sorrow whose hankering for pleasure has gone and who is free from attachment fear and anger is known as a silent sage of steady mind

tr.text: One who is undisturbed by sorrow whose hankering for pleasure has gone and who is free from attachment fear and anger is known as a silent sage of steady mind

sw.text: One who is undisturbed by sorrow whose hankering for pleasure has gone and who is free from attachment fear and anger is known as a silent sage of steady mind

he.text: One who is not attached to anything in this world who neither enjoys nor resents auspiciousness or inauspiciousness—his חוכמה is firmly established

ar.text: One who is not attached to anything in this world who neither enjoys nor resents auspiciousness or inauspiciousness—his الحكمة is firmly established

tr.text: One who is not attached to anything in this world who neither enjoys nor resents auspiciousness or inauspiciousness—his bilgelik is firmly established

sw.text: One who is not attached to anything in this world who neither enjoys nor resents auspiciousness or inauspiciousness—his busara is firmly established

he.text: When one’s senses are controlled and totally withdrawn from their objects like the limbs of the tortoise withdrawn in its shell his חוכמה is firmly established

ar.text: When one’s senses are controlled and totally withdrawn from their objects like the limbs of the tortoise withdrawn in its shell his الحكمة is firmly established

tr.text: When one’s senses are controlled and totally withdrawn from their objects like the limbs of the tortoise withdrawn in its shell his bilgelik is firmly established

sw.text: When one’s senses are controlled and totally withdrawn from their objects like the limbs of the tortoise withdrawn in its shell his busara is firmly established

he.text: The embodied being may renounce the sense objects but the taste for them remains But that too vanishes for the man of חוכמה by his vision of the Supreme

ar.text: The embodied being may renounce the sense objects but the taste for them remains But that too vanishes for the man of الحكمة by his vision of the Supreme

tr.text: The embodied being may renounce the sense objects but the taste for them remains But that too vanishes for the man of bilgelik by his vision of the Supreme

sw.text: The embodied being may renounce the sense objects but the taste for them remains But that too vanishes for the man of busara by his vision of the Supreme

he.text: And yet O son of Kuntī the turbulent senses can forcibly plunder the mind of even the wise person striving for liberation

ar.text: And yet O son of Kuntī the turbulent senses can forcibly plunder the mind of even the wise person striving for liberation

tr.text: And yet O son of Kuntī the turbulent senses can forcibly plunder the mind of even the wise person striving for liberation

sw.text: And yet O son of Kuntī the turbulent senses can forcibly plunder the mind of even the wise person striving for liberation

he.text: Devoted to Me the bhakti-yogī brings his senses under proper control Firmly established is the חוכמה of one whose senses are controlled

ar.text: Devoted to Me the bhakti-yogī brings his senses under proper control Firmly established is the الحكمة of one whose senses are controlled

tr.text: Devoted to Me the bhakti-yogī brings his senses under proper control Firmly established is the bilgelik of one whose senses are controlled

sw.text: Devoted to Me the bhakti-yogī brings his senses under proper control Firmly established is the busara of one whose senses are controlled

he.text: But one whose thoughts dwell on the objects of the senses becomes attached to them From attachment desire is born and from desire anger arises

ar.text: But one whose thoughts dwell on the objects of the senses becomes attached to them From attachment desire is born and from desire anger arises

tr.text: But one whose thoughts dwell on the objects of the senses becomes attached to them From attachment desire is born and from desire anger arises

sw.text: But one whose thoughts dwell on the objects of the senses becomes attached to them From attachment desire is born and from desire anger arises

he.text: Anger gives rise to delusion and the power of delusion causes forgetfulness forgetfulness destroys the intelligence and when the intelligence is lost one loses all

ar.text: Anger gives rise to delusion and the power of delusion causes forgetfulness forgetfulness destroys the intelligence and when the intelligence is lost one loses all

tr.text: Anger gives rise to delusion and the power of delusion causes forgetfulness forgetfulness destroys the intelligence and when the intelligence is lost one loses all

sw.text: Anger gives rise to delusion and the power of delusion causes forgetfulness forgetfulness destroys the intelligence and when the intelligence is lost one loses all

he.text: But the integrated person who can control his mind at will and who free from attraction and repulsion accepts his worldly necessities through his controlled senses attains tranquillity

ar.text: But the integrated person who can control his mind at will and who free from attraction and repulsion accepts his worldly necessities through his controlled senses attains tranquillity

tr.text: But the integrated person who can control his mind at will and who free from attraction and repulsion accepts his worldly necessities through his controlled senses attains tranquillity

sw.text: But the integrated person who can control his mind at will and who free from attraction and repulsion accepts his worldly necessities through his controlled senses attains tranquillity

he.text: When one attains a peaceful heart all his sorrows are dispelled The tranquil person’s חוכמה swiftly becomes steadfast

ar.text: When one attains a peaceful heart all his sorrows are dispelled The tranquil person’s الحكمة swiftly becomes steadfast

tr.text: When one attains a peaceful heart all his sorrows are dispelled The tranquil person’s bilgelik swiftly becomes steadfast

sw.text: When one attains a peaceful heart all his sorrows are dispelled The tranquil person’s busara swiftly becomes steadfast

he.text: A person bereft of self-control can have neither חוכמה nor מדיטציה Without מדיטציה there is no שלום and without שלום where is the hope for אושר

ar.text: A person bereft of self-control can have neither الحكمة nor التأمل Without التأمل there is no السلام and without السلام where is the hope for السعادة

tr.text: A person bereft of self-control can have neither bilgelik nor meditasyon Without meditasyon there is no huzur and without huzur where is the hope for mutluluk

sw.text: A person bereft of self-control can have neither busara nor kutafakari Without kutafakari there is no amani and without amani where is the hope for furaha

he.text: As a boat on the ocean is thrown off course by an unfavourable wind similarly the חוכמה of a person lacking self-control is carried away by even one of the roaming senses that the mind might follow

ar.text: As a boat on the ocean is thrown off course by an unfavourable wind similarly the الحكمة of a person lacking self-control is carried away by even one of the roaming senses that the mind might follow

tr.text: As a boat on the ocean is thrown off course by an unfavourable wind similarly the bilgelik of a person lacking self-control is carried away by even one of the roaming senses that the mind might follow

sw.text: As a boat on the ocean is thrown off course by an unfavourable wind similarly the busara of a person lacking self-control is carried away by even one of the roaming senses that the mind might follow

he.text: Therefore O subduer of the enemy one whose senses are fully withdrawn from their objects is a person of firmly established חוכמה

ar.text: Therefore O subduer of the enemy one whose senses are fully withdrawn from their objects is a person of firmly established الحكمة

tr.text: Therefore O subduer of the enemy one whose senses are fully withdrawn from their objects is a person of firmly established bilgelik

sw.text: Therefore O subduer of the enemy one whose senses are fully withdrawn from their objects is a person of firmly established busara

he.text: That which is day for the self-controlled sage is night for all beings and that which is day for all beings is night for the sage who sees The living beings absorbed in mundane life are asleep to the spiritual joy of the realised soul who remains indifferent to their quest for the pleasures of the senses devoid of spiritual joy

ar.text: That which is day for the self-controlled sage is night for all beings and that which is day for all beings is night for the sage who sees The living beings absorbed in mundane life are asleep to the spiritual joy of the realised soul who remains indifferent to their quest for the pleasures of the senses devoid of spiritual joy

tr.text: That which is day for the self-controlled sage is night for all beings and that which is day for all beings is night for the sage who sees The living beings absorbed in mundane life are asleep to the spiritual joy of the realised soul who remains indifferent to their quest for the pleasures of the senses devoid of spiritual joy

sw.text: That which is day for the self-controlled sage is night for all beings and that which is day for all beings is night for the sage who sees The living beings absorbed in mundane life are asleep to the spiritual joy of the realised soul who remains indifferent to their quest for the pleasures of the senses devoid of spiritual joy

he.text: As the ocean remains full and unchanged though many rivers and streams enter into it the person of firmly established חוכמה attains שלום despite all desires entering him But a person who nurtures desires cannot know שלום

ar.text: As the ocean remains full and unchanged though many rivers and streams enter into it the person of firmly established الحكمة attains السلام despite all desires entering him But a person who nurtures desires cannot know السلام

tr.text: As the ocean remains full and unchanged though many rivers and streams enter into it the person of firmly established bilgelik attains huzur despite all desires entering him But a person who nurtures desires cannot know huzur

sw.text: As the ocean remains full and unchanged though many rivers and streams enter into it the person of firmly established busara attains amani despite all desires entering him But a person who nurtures desires cannot know amani

he.text: Only a person who lives his life abandoning all desires hankering ego and possessiveness can know real שלום

ar.text: Only a person who lives his life abandoning all desires hankering ego and possessiveness can know real السلام

tr.text: Only a person who lives his life abandoning all desires hankering ego and possessiveness can know real huzur

sw.text: Only a person who lives his life abandoning all desires hankering ego and possessiveness can know real amani

he.text: O Pārtha this is realisation of the Absolute attaining which a person is freed from the delusion of mundane existence Even at the time of death only a momentary attainment of this state leads one to the divine abode

ar.text: O Pārtha this is realisation of the Absolute attaining which a person is freed from the delusion of mundane existence Even at the time of death only a momentary attainment of this state leads one to the divine abode

tr.text: O Pārtha this is realisation of the Absolute attaining which a person is freed from the delusion of mundane existence Even at the time of death only a momentary attainment of this state leads one to the divine abode

sw.text: O Pārtha this is realisation of the Absolute attaining which a person is freed from the delusion of mundane existence Even at the time of death only a momentary attainment of this state leads one to the divine abode
